programmes.show_description: Ultras: Sandi Toksvig presents an ultra-interesting episode with Jordan Gray, Jason Manford, Sara Pascoe and Alan Davies.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Richard Ayoade hosts with rail union leader Mick Lynch and comedian Roisin Conaty joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as guest panellists to delve into the news.
Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Jo Brand, Lucy Martin, Amol Rajan and Joe Wilkinson for more extraordinary stories.
Mel and Lou are joined by Ross Kemp, Shirley Ballas and Rosie Jones as Shirley divulges her revenge on a cheating husband and Ross spills all on a prison-based prank.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Kalahari: Simon Reeve travels across the Kalahari desert in southern Africa, going on a hunt with a group of San trackers before heading to the oasis of the Okavango Delta.
Royston: Sparks fly in the confrontational reality series - quite literally this time, as a storage bin goes up in smoke! A mystery object under tarpaulin causes a catfight.
Newcastle: Not everything is black and white in Newcastle, as savvy bidders gamble on unseen storage units in the hit reality show. Feisty new girl Ione clashes with Shaun.
Customs and Border Protection officers are stunned when they discover more than $2million of cocaine mixed in with gallons of household paint and bottles of beer.
Vigilant Customs and Border Protection officers uncover 5,000 rounds of ammunition being smuggled across Hidalgo Bridge into Mexico.
Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, and Jason Dawe are joined by special guest Jay Kay as they review the Ford Focus RS, insider dealing, and the RS1800.
Ross Kemp joins the guys as Jeremy Clarkson road tests the Aston Martin DB7, Jason Dawe checks out two second-hand cars, and Richard Hammond looks at the Citroen DS.
Paul and Bob go lake fishing in Wiltshire and are up before sunrise to give themselves a fighting chance of catching a crucian carp, one of the UK's shyest species.
Bob and Paul revisit the River Wye in Herefordshire - having fished the Middle Wye for barbel before, this time they head to the spectacular Lower Wye to fish for chub.
Slow Food: The bikers celebrate British food. For something delicious, it's worth using the best ingredients and cooking them to perfection - however long it takes.
Beer and Cider: The lovable bikers explore how our love affair with beer and cider has evolved over time. They also visit a cider festival, where they press their own.
Comedian Lost Voice Guy - also known as Lee Ridley - joins Robson in County Durham to explore the benefits of the great outdoors and go for a stroll down memory lane.
Kalahari: Simon Reeve travels across the Kalahari desert in southern Africa, going on a hunt with a group of San trackers before heading to the oasis of the Okavango Delta.
Simon Reeve begins an ambitious journey travelling the entire length of the Americas - in this first leg, Simon travels from Alaska and down through Canada to Vancouver.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Checkroom: Counter Terrorism Policing foil an Islamist terror plot, and the CPS Special Crime Unit bring those responsible for a fatal crash on the M62 to justice.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Napoleon: Manchester police take down a gang of teen car thieves while the Serious Fraud Office unravels a 400-million-pound global fraud in the steel industry.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Richard Ayoade hosts with rail union leader Mick Lynch and comedian Roisin Conaty joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as guest panellists to delve into the news.
Ridiculous: Sandi Toksvig hosts an absolutely ridiculous episode with Alan Davies, Maisie Adam, David Mitchell and Holly Walsh.
Dara O Briain hosts as guests Rob Beckett, Milton Jones, Romesh Ranganathan and Holly Walsh pillory the news-makers. Worth watching just for Paul McCartney's meat-free rap.
Another satirical summit as Dara and co scale a mountain of news and plant their flippant flags at the top. With James Acaster, Ed Byrne, Sara Pascoe and Josh Widdicombe.
More cutting-edge stand-up from the iconic theatre in London. The magnificent Jen Brister introduces comedians Esther Manito and Adam Rowe to the stage.
Ufology: Sandi Toksvig dusts off her tinfoil hat for a look at ufology with Cally Beaton, Nish Kumar, Tom Ward and Alan Davies.
Stop Saying Lice!: Infectiously hilarious sitcom. Johnny tries to stop a spread of lice in the town, and Moira tries to get out of having a garden named after her.
Friends & Family: More comedy with the warm-hearted Rose clan. Johnny and Moira look for a location for a family portrait, and David hosts a soft launch for the new store.
Grad Night: One final third-series fling with the lovably backwater town. Johnny and Stevie celebrate having a fully-booked hotel, and David and Patrick go on a date.
Dead Guy in Room 4: The opening episode in the fourth series of the smash-hit sitcom. Moira feels responsible for a hotel guest's death, and David and Patrick make their romance official.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Food Festivals: Another helping of top British grub with the bikers. The duo take a look at all the food festivals in the calendar that allow the British to indulge.
Britain's most talented detectives pursue two gunmen killers in Northamptonshire, distraction burglars in sleepy Kent, and a multi-million-pound footwear scam in Manchester.
Britain's most talented detectives pursue a violent drug gang in Gloucester, an aggressive mugger in sleepy Kent, and a gang of men stealing Britain's history in Cheshire.
Documentary series. John Mackie takes on a hellish loco restoration job, Wilmar Sugar race to repair cyclone-destroyed tracks, and an upstart freight company takes on Broken Hill.

programmes.show_name: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programmes.show_description: Rick Stein's gastronomic journey brings him to northern Greece and the historic city of Ioannina, where he tastes a traditional veal stifado and kotopita.
A tip-off unearths a vast shipment of heroin and cocaine disguised as garbage bags, and officers respond to a medical emergency and deal with a drunken US Marshal.
More than 130 packets of cocaine are found hidden in the seats of a bus, a car explodes in the middle of the bridge, and a retiring officer makes his final bust.
Engineering enthusiast Guy Martin renovates a sawmill in Wensleydale in North Yorkshire and then uses it to make a replica of the first pedal-powered bicycle.
High-octane entertainment. Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond are on location with the Sweeney movie and taking charge of the film's climactic car chase, and Ryan Reynolds guests.
Fasten your seatbelts for more motoring mayhem. The boys embark on a project to build mobility scooters that can tackle the British countryside.

programmes.show_name: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programmes.show_description: Rick Stein's gastronomic journey brings him to northern Greece and the historic city of Ioannina, where he tastes a traditional veal stifado and kotopita.
Food Festivals: Another helping of top British grub with the bikers. The duo take a look at all the food festivals in the calendar that allow the British to indulge.
More Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with more unseen material from guests including Charlie Brooker, Johnny Marr, Shazia Mirza and Craig Charles.
TV presenter Gemma Cairney, comedy actor Tim Key, and sports hosts Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their general knowledge skills with the ever-genial Pointless host this time.
TV presenter Charlotte Hawkins and comedians David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, and Sophie Duker test themselves against the best-selling author and Pointless friend's fiendish games.
'U' Animals: Sandi Toksvig, Rhod Gilbert, Lara Ricote, Ahir Shah and Alan Davies look at some animals beginning with 'U'.
New. In a time where Donald Trump can be president, who better to dissect the news than Frankie Boyle? In this edition, Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe.
Zoe Lyons, Milton Jones, Rob Beckett and Ed Byrne join masters of mockery Dara O Briain, Andy Parsons and Hugh Dennis on the popular panel show that roughs up the news.
